Title: Intra-Kernel Reallocation of Proteins in Maize Depends on VP1-Mediated Scutellum Development and Nutrient Assimilation

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Seed storage protein (GLB1); lysine-enriched storage protein pool
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Embryo nutrient storage and amino acid reservoir; contributes to seed nutritional quality
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Embryo scutellum, especially middle-to-upper scutellum; protein storage vacuoles
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Embryo-specific; begins ~20 DAP, peaks ~28 DAP, mainly in scutellum near shoot apex
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced in aRNAi embryos from 24–36 DAP; strongest in middle-to-upper scutellum
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mass spectrometry of induced bands; glb1 glb2 double-null mutant; immunoblotting; RT-qPCR; RNA in situ hybridization; immunogold labeling; genetic analysis with aRNAi; protein quantification
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes embryo storage protein accumulation and is the predominant protein induced during endosperm-to-embryo protein reallocation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GLB1-L and GLB1-S are the major embryo proteins induced when endosperm α-zeins are suppressed. 2. Loss of Glb1/Glb2 reduces embryo protein content, showing GLBs are the dominant amino-acid storage capacity in embryo. 3. Glb1 expression is transcriptionally upregulated in aRNAi embryos and depends on VP1.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker and target for improving embryo protein quality and potentially lysine content in Quality Protein Maize; promoter can serve as reporter for nutrient reallocation studies
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GLB1 is proteolytically processed into large and small subunits; induced bands 1L and 1S correspond to GLB1-L and GLB1-S

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Embryo scutellum / embryo protein bodies or storage vacuoles
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Embryo-expressed; temporal pattern similar to Glb1 but lower abundance
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression initiated earlier in aRNAi embryos and later maintained better than wild type; undetectable in vp1 embryos
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: glb1 glb2 mutant sequencing; immunoblotting; RT-qPCR; genetic analysis with aRNAi and vp1
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to embryo storage protein accumulation but is less abundant than GLB1
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GLB2 is a minor embryo globulin relative to GLB1. 2. Its transcript/protein are altered by aRNAi and abolished in vp1. 3. Together with GLB1 it supports embryo protein storage capacity.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate supportive target for improving embryo protein reserves together with Glb1
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Less abundant than GLB1 in wild type and aRNAi embryos

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: B3-domain TF with A1 activation, B2, and B3 domains
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ABA signaling; interacts with SnRK2 kinases; endosperm nutritional status/proteome rebalancing
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts with ABA signaling components; likely functions with other TFs for promoter activation
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for effector in Dual-LUC; native promoters of Glb1, Glb2 and other nutrient-metabolism genes tested as reporters
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in developing embryo; at 16 DAP across embryo but stronger in scutellum near endosperm; at 24 DAP mainly in scutellum near MES and shoot apex
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Not transcriptionally induced in aRNAi, but spatially associated with scutellum differentiation; required for target gene activation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: vp1 mutant phenotyping; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; immunoblotting; RNA in situ hybridization; LCI interaction assay; Dual-LUC transactivation assays; genetic interaction with aRNAi; sugar quantification
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Embryo nutrient assimilation, storage protein accumulation, sulfur/amino acid/carbohydrate metabolism, scutellum development
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Glb1; Glb2; Em/Esp5; Oleosin Zm-II; ZmBAM3; diverse aRNAi-induced genes in carbohydrate, amino acid, sulfur metabolism and stress response
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates Glb1 and Glb2 and multiple embryo-expressed nutrient metabolism genes; required for aRNAi-mediated protein reallocation
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of aRNAi embryos (16 and 24 DAP) and vp1 embryos (20 DAP); NCBI accession stated but specific ID not provided
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Likely feedforward coupling with ABA signaling during embryo maturation; not directly established
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ABA application in Dual-LUC assays strongly enhances transactivation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation coordinated with ABA signaling; indirect or assisted DNA-binding mechanism
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: vp1 causes severe developmental defects, reduced storage reserves, vivipary, impaired nutrient assimilation, and broad pleiotropic transcriptional changes
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Embryo storage proteins and nutrient reserve accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: VP1 did not bind tested ABRE/G-box probes in EMSA, suggesting transactivation may require cofactors; central regulator rather than direct simple DNA-binding model
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VP1 is essential for scutellum development and embryo nutrient assimilation. 2. vp1 abolishes GLB accumulation and prevents aRNAi-induced protein reallocation from endosperm to embryo. 3. VP1 transactivates Glb1 and multiple nutrient metabolism genes, especially with ABA.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulator for manipulating seed nutrient allocation and embryo filling, but deployment requires caution due to pleiotropic effects on dormancy/vivipary and development
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master regulator required for embryo protein reallocation and scutellum nutrient-storage capacity
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for overaccumulation of embryo globulins and for endosperm-to-embryo protein/nutrient allocation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports seed maturation, embryo nutrient storage, and nutritional quality

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Endosperm zein storage protein gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Most zein genes, especially 22-kD α-zein and β-zein-related program
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates zein accumulation program in endosperm; mutation reduces zeins and indirectly increases embryo GLBs
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Opaque kernel phenotype and altered endosperm proteome
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used mainly as comparative mutant; not the main mechanistic focus of this study
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. o2 reduces α- and β-zein synthesis in endosperm. 2. Similar to aRNAi, o2 indirectly induces embryo GLB accumulation. 3. Serves as comparison showing embryo responds to endosperm proteome perturbation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Classic quality protein regulator, but in this paper mainly a reference comparator rather than the central subject
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strong determinant of endosperm zein reduction, indirectly altering embryo protein allocation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation reduces endosperm zeins and indirectly promotes embryo GLB accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed storage reserve affecting protein quality
